name: QNI : High Inbound Emails Containing Attachments From External Host
notes:
This rule triggers when an address outside the organization sends numerous emails containing attachments. This could indicate an attempt to deliver a malware by targeting a large number of recipients.

Note: The threshold should be adapted to the size of the company
conditions_text: when a flow matches any of the following BB:CategoryDefinition: Mail Applications in Flows
when the flow matches File Name is not N/A
when the flow matches REFERENCESETCONTAINS('Corporate Email Domains',REPLACEALL('.*\@(.+?)\>?$',"originating user",'$1')) == FALSE AQL filter query
when at least 100 flows are seen with the same Originating User in 5 minutes
thresholds: when at least 100 flows are seen with the same Originating User in 5 minutes
TD_list: APPLY QNI : High Inbound Emails Containing Attachments From External Host on flows which are detected by the LOCAL system
AND when a flow matches any of the following BB:CategoryDefinition: Mail Applications in Flows
AND when the flow matches File Name is not N/A
AND when the flow matches REFERENCESETCONTAINS('Corporate Email Domains',REPLACEALL('.*\@(.+?)\>?$',"originating user",'$1')) == FALSE AQL filter query
AND when at least 100 flows are seen with the same Originating User in 5 minutes
RED_list: Event name: QNI : High Inbound Emails Containing Attachments From External Host
Event description: Detects when numerous people in the organization receive emails containing attachments from the same external sender.
Force the dispatched event to create a NEW offense, select the offense using Source IP
This information should contribute to the name of the associated offense(s)
